records.ety: [F. stratagme (cf. Sp. estratagema, It. stratagemma), L. strategema, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to be leader of an army, fr. <?/ a general; <?/ an army (probably as being spread out; cf. Stratum) + <?/ to lead. See Agent.]

defs: An artifice or trick in war for deceiving the enemy; hence, in general, artifice; deceptive device; secret plot; evil machination.
quotes: Fit for treasons, stratagems, and spoils.
Those oft are stratagems which error seem, Nor is it Homer nods, but we that dream.
